GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (Istanbul) - Yes, thank you.
Dear friends, we are talking about an extremely vital issue for Türkiye. Türkiye's population was 44 million in 1980, today we are 86 million, roughly speaking, we are the children of a country whose population increases by nearly 1 million per year. Since humans are biological beings and must be fed, we will either feed this increasing population with products produced in these lands or import them. Therefore, let us first underline that repeating the previous year in agriculture is clearly a regression. I would be happy if you listen to me like this. More than a political party conflict, this will be a time to clearly reveal the facts and decipher the tales that have been told to adults so far.
Dear Minister, let me tell you this: I tried to read your 2026 budget speech carefully. Do you know, I was able to draw 4 sentences. I feel great sadness about this. And I drew them all so I could answer them. You are doing once again what you always do. What did you say? "We are 1st in Europe and 7th in the world." You said right?
NİLGÜN ÖK (Denizli) - Yes.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Madam, could you please excuse me? Stay calm, if you love your God, stay calm. Look, I'm explaining it nicely.
"We are 1st in Europe and 7th in the world." you said. This is a narrative of agricultural success, isn't it? Well, let me tell you: You are net foreign dependent in the foreign trade of agricultural raw materials. Let me tell you. A classification called "ISIC Rev.3"
did you hear? "I didn't hear it." If you say so, I say hear it and research it. “We have heard it and yet we distort it.” If you say so, it would be a shame. Why am I trying to explain? There are food products, you look at their imports and exports; There is also foreign trade in agricultural raw materials, you look at their imports and exports. Türkiye is a net exporter in foreign trade of food products and a net importer in foreign trade of agricultural raw materials. Now, why don't you tell this truth? Do you think that by not telling this truth you will make Türkiye soar in agriculture?
Look, I will not tell you anything other than TUIK figures - as my friends said, TUIK figures - I will even give you the Ministry figures.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - We have a net current account surplus in agriculture.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Calm down my friend, I'll tell you right away.
For example, you are 87 percent self-sufficient in wheat. Türkiye produces 20 million tons of wheat, right? I will give you the figures for 2003-2025. Türkiye says: "I produced 443 million tons of wheat." "International Grains Council" do you know this: International Grains Council, you know, of course you know because even though you are a journalist, you have been working at the Ministry of Agriculture for two and a half years, you must have learned it.
MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FORESTRY İBRAHİM YUMAKLI - That is also wrong, I am not a journalist.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - You are not a journalist, you were not a member of the Board of Directors at GÜBRETAŞ, that is true.
Let me tell you now: You claim that you produced 443 million tons. I was the Chairman of the Chamber of Agricultural Engineers for many years. I know very well how these production calculations are made. The International Grain Council tells you: "You produced 378 million tons in this period of time.

"How many million tons are there? There are 65 million tons. I asked this to all the ministers. Oh, don't people ask? "What about IGC, you say that I produce 65 million tons less. By what method do you find this? You don't say that because you are trying to create a gross national product story by exaggerating wheat production figures.
I will give another figure, maybe it will convince you. During your rule, wheat cultivation areas decreased from 9 million 100 thousand hectares to 7 million 24 thousand hectares. In other words, wheat cultivation areas decreased by 2 million hectares. Do you know this number or not? Bureaucrats sit there. I'm asking, do you know the TURKSTAT figure or not? If you know, have you investigated why? Why are people shrinking their wheat acreage? Let me tell you: There was a tractor here a while ago, this tractor can hold 80 liters of diesel. If you try to fill 80 liters of diesel with diesel for 60 liras, you will pay 5 thousand liras for 1 tank of diesel in the tractor. This man will put diesel fuel in his tractor for 5 thousand TL, produce wheat in dry land with an efficiency of 300 kilograms per decare, and earn money from this, right?
Have we ever heard of "internal terms of trade"? It is an economic term. If you don't care about the internal terms of trade, you will be faced with these figures. "We produce 20 million tons." The numbers, as I just said, are doubtful, but let's say it is 19 million tons, it does not matter what happens. Türkiye was a granary, wasn't it? How much do we import? 10 million tons. Look, you are getting ready immediately, I know very well. "Yes, we import this, but we sell pasta, brother, we sell flour, we sell semolina."
YAŞAR KIRKPINAR (İzmir) - Bravo!
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (Istanbul) - Do you know the equivalence coefficient in wheat? I joined the Soil Products Office as an expert. A significant part of my life was spent working at TMO. The amount of wheat required for the export of all these is 7 million tons. TMO's General Manager is a valuable bureaucrat there. These calculations were made and published as an article. What does this mean: You have made Türkiye dependent on foreign sources for wheat by 3 million tons, but you do not accept this. If you don't accept this, does the truth change? It doesn't change. The picture is that clear.
Your self-sufficiency is 67 percent in barley. Let me give you a corn figure: 77 percent. You produced 120 million tons of corn and imported 35 million tons of corn. The money you paid for corn imports alone during your time in power is over 8 billion dollars. Your self-sufficiency is 75 percent in rice, 59 percent in sunflower. Shall I give you the production figure, again for twenty-three years? These are important figures, Mr. Minister. You produced 32 million tons of sunflower, 13 million
You imported tons and paid 7 billion dollars just for sunflowers.
Soy. Are there any Adana MPs here, friends?
AYHAN BARUT (Adana) - We are here.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (Istanbul) - Adana, right? Soybean is 6 percent, your self-sufficiency rate is 6 percent. You import more than 4 million tons of soy, the amount of soy you can produce is not even 200 thousand tons. Do you know how much you paid for soy? Hold tight: You paid 19.4 billion dollars just for soybean imports.

Let me tell you something: What is the importance of soy? You know, you brag, "We are very good at poultry, we are very good at chicken." TRUE; The only sub-sector doing well is poultry farming. Let me summarize poultry farming for you with two things: First, especially in meat breeding, you are 100 percent dependent on foreign sources. If you do not buy a broiler breeder, you cannot produce chickens. Secondly, without soy, you cannot produce chicken. That is, if you do not buy soy from outside, you cannot produce chicken. The issue is that clear, that obvious. You tell us stories, tales for adults: "We are 1st in Europe, 7th in the world." Well, these are the numbers.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - It's not a story, it's reality.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - I will tell you about the foreign trade of agricultural raw materials in ISIC Rev.3, see ISIC Rev.3. You had a deficit of $5.7 billion in 2021, $7.6 billion in 2022. Look, I always talk in numbers. You had a deficit of 4.5 billion dollars in 2023 and 4.6 billion dollars in 2024 in foreign trade of agricultural raw materials. Well, what can I say roughly: In the foreign trade of agricultural raw materials, you pay 10 billion dollars for imports in a normal year. How many times should we multiply this? Should we multiply it by 42? In other words, you pay 420 billion TL for agricultural raw material imports in a normal year. Half of this is deficit, that is, half is foreign trade deficit. That means 200 billion TL. You are throwing away money just because you cannot grow basic agricultural products in these lands, where agriculture began, and then you say: "We are 1st in Europe." Well, I'm actually looking at you like this, I'm looking at you while you're talking, I mean, are you saying this with belief, are you saying these by seeing these numbers, or are they giving you these numbers and you're just reading these numbers here without internalizing them?
MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FORESTRY İBRAHİM YUMAKLI - International figures.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - I do not say these to be disrespectful, these are the facts. We all put on our ties and sat here. We cannot fool each other, especially the nation, the picture is that clear.
Now, "Cattle existence has increased by 71 percent; it has reached 17.7 million." etcetera. I listened with amazement, amazement. Why with astonishment? I will compare 1982-2025, forty-five years. How much did the population increase? The population increased from 44 to 86, right? It had increased by roughly 1 million each year. Cattle wealth was 16 million in 1980 and 16 million today. Doesn't this seem strange to you? Can you say that yes, the cattle population has not changed, but the productivity has increased so much that we have eliminated the problem? Productivity increased, of course, but not enough to close the gap.
Let me tell you something else: Aren't buffalo cattle? This country had 1 million buffaloes in 1980, today it has 162 thousand buffalos. 8 out of 10 buffalos have disappeared, brother! 49 million.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - Why do you compare it with the 80's?
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Well, I say that the population is increasing by 1 million every year, so we are doing something together.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - Our comparison is not with the 80's.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - As you were saying before, let's listen to the conversations and respond. If you love God, answer me with numbers like me on my head, okay, on my head.
I will say the existence of sheep.

ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - I will give it, we will give it to all of them.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - I will tell about the existence of sheep.
I wish you would give it to these elders so that we can refute the fairy tales, share the facts and move on.
Presence of sheep.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - I hope you will stay.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Oh, we are here, brother, we are here, we are here, we are at the General Assembly, can I explain. Let me spend my time explaining agriculture to people, okay?
This country had 49 million sheep in 1980, today it has 44 million sheep; He had 19 million goats, 10 million goats, so we missed 1 in every 2 goats. Then you tell us that our animal population has increased, I don't know why. Now I'm sure you're thinking: Our red meat production has increased, right? You will say so. Let me give you a figure: Türkiye produced 1 million 70 thousand tons of red meat in 2020. Then in 2021, it suddenly started producing 1 million 952 thousand tons of red meat. Knows a little math
I ask everyone: Does a trend in a living population that has not changed for twenty years suddenly increase the number from 1 million tons to 2 million tons in one year? Do you think you can fool the country by playing with statistics? This is something that was done before your Ministry, but it reveals how unreliable today's statistics in Türkiye are. Your red meat production also decreased from 2023 to 2024; Now you produce 2.1 million tons. Let me tell you: You have made Türkiye the country with the lowest per capita consumption of red meat in all of Europe. In Türkiye, 1 kilogram of red meat does not enter the homes of millions of people. I'm telling you sincerely; I ask the retired person I came across while working in Anatolia, "What is the price of 1 kilogram of red meat and 1 kilogram of minced meat?" I swear, "We don't know." they say. Does this really upset you, Mr. Minister, after all these imports?
Let me give you another number. What is the reason why you reached these figures in red meat production in Türkiye? Because we are agricultural engineers and our teachers tell us: "The meat issue is the grass issue." they say. So, what happened to pasture in Türkiye? When you came to power, there were 17 million hectares of pasture, 17 million hectares. You say that there are 13 million hectares of pasture today. If there were 13 million hectares of pasture in this country, I would tear up my agricultural engineer diploma; I say this clearly. Well, let me tell you, on paper, "We made determinations, restrictions and allocations." you say, right? I swear, you go and say that you throw the fertilizers on the pastures. There is no increase in the quality of a pasture in any of those places, only some people become rich; We know all this very well. There is neither detection nor restriction nor allocation nor an increase in the quality of pasture assets; Let's not deceive each other.
Feed production. I just gave an example from soy. Türkiye produces 30 million tons of feed, 30 million tons; Beautiful. 15 million tons of this is based on imported raw materials, brother. If you cannot bring 15 million tons of raw materials imported from abroad, there will be no feed to feed your animals; It's that simple. So don't tell us "We are 1st in Europe." so and so; These are the numbers, these are the facts.
What did you do next?

Look, when we were the president of the chamber, an import of 19 thousand tons was going to be opened, we raised a fuss saying "19 thousand tons cannot be imported, do not kill the livestock breeders inside." saying. Now, how much do you import? Should I give you some figures? You import 500-600 thousand tons of livestock - livestock - 120 thousand heads of butcher animals, and 150 thousand tons of red meat. More than 3 billion dollars is the money you spend only on imports.
Let me tell you something, let me tell you something, Mr. Minister, I will tell you 4 names. I researched, your name is not mentioned in the import lobbies, but pay attention to the following names: Mücahit, Bülent, Mustafa, Bilal; pay attention to these. Be careful of these, what they do will be upon you. Sooner or later their actions will be revealed; They earn big money, but Türkiye is becoming dependent on red meat, this is unacceptable. Oh, let me tell you, this Meat and Milk Institution General Manager; I wish you hadn't undertaken it like this because you were saying. Explanations were made, at least you yourself were not saying anything here; you have undertaken here. Well, there is a famous proverb: They made the fox the manager of the chicken coop and said, "How much salary do you want?" They asked, but he couldn't answer because he laughed. Well, the man has a company in Hungary; Would that company also appear to be importing on paper? So, doesn't the one who steals the minaret adjust his cover?
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - That was your claim.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - So you have no doubts in this whole picture? For example, if I were the owner of a build-sell company, would you make me TOKİ General Manager? You shouldn't do it, you shouldn't do it, brother.
TURHAN ÇÖMEZ (Balıkesir) - Mr. Gökhan, his certificate is here, his certificate is here.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - These are matters of political ethics. At least "When this situation comes to light, we dismiss it until this situation is clarified." if you said. Well, it is already unsuccessful, it has already overwhelmed the country with imports, and although it has already drowned the country with imports, red meat production and consumption has decreased, and on top of that
This man has been trading this for years, he has an international company to trade, and here you are saying: "What's in it?" What if it's "What's in this?", what are you looking for? What are you looking for?
MINISTER OF AGRICULTURE AND FORESTRY İBRAHİM YUMAKLI - I did not say such a thing.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - Mr. Minister responded to the allegation that he sold meat to the Institution.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - How could political ethics be any different? So therefore, all of these, look at the whole.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - The Minister responded to your claim that meat was sold to the Meat and Milk Institution.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Let me say this once: If you say "Let the court get the result." If you say "I am referring this to the Commission." you say.
I say it once again: I have listed 4 names for you, pay attention to these 4 names, pay attention to the enrichment of those 4 names, because because of them, the country has become unable to produce meat and consume meat.
Finally, I will say something, a legal expression: "Conflict of law" Have you ever heard of it? So "Conflict of interest", "conflict of interest". Dear Minister, I do not say these to disrespect you, the word "conflict of interest" applies exactly to this.

A man cannot be made manager of a chicken coop if he is a fox. At least there is a conflict of interest here, it's that simple. If we're talking about this here - I'll say it once again - as if we put on our ties in the morning and there's no problem, then what else are we going to talk about? There is no need to talk about anything else. The issue is so clear, there is clearly a rot. This decay is not at a point where it can continue.
Let's talk about food inspections. What do you say? There will be 1 million 329 thousand cases of food poisoning in 2024, 1 million 160 thousand cases in 2025, and 1,256 cases of food poisoning in the last month alone. The symptoms that occur in food poisoning are just the tip of the iceberg. There is an incredible reliability problem in the field of food in Türkiye. So, what's the reason for this? Yes, "We are 1st in Europe and 7th in the world." You say, I will show you an area where you are the 1st in the world, you are the 1st in the world. These are food inflation figures. You are by far the 1st in the world in food inflation. There are 5 countries after you: Estonia, Japan, Slovenia, Latvia and Czechia. The total inflation of these 5 countries is the total inflation of the food you produce alone. Oh, agriculture started in these lands ten thousand years ago, brother! Ten thousand years ago, agriculture began in these lands, and you condemned people to starvation in this country. Why don't you ever mention this number? Ya, you have a presentation, there is something here. "Food inflation has increased despite all the efforts of the Ministry of Agriculture. We are taking the following measures." Why don't you say?
Oh, really, Berat Albayrak was building Sera Inc. to prevent food inflation; What happened to that job, what happened to that job? Production in Türkiye was not enough, so you were renting land from Sudan; What really happened, what happened? This was all a lie and disappeared? In Türkiye, you were making electric tractors to reduce costs, and you were entering the fields with shoe covers; What happened, what happened?
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Dear Good Morning, I give two minutes of additional time.
Let's complete it.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Overshoes continue to stand, but where is the electric tractor?
Now I will say one more thing, I will tell you about two regulations; The first is the Biodiesel Regulation and the second is the Plant Protection and Fumigation Regulation.
Dear Minister, I am perhaps one of the people who uses phosphine gas the most in Türkiye at the Turkish Grain Board. Phosphine is used in warehouses as a fumigation agent. If you use phosphine to spray a hotel while others are staying in the next room, you are a criminal. You remember the insect family, right? 2 children, 1 mother, 1 father; o 4 people died. Whose sleep will this disturb? Will it disturb the Minister of Health's sleep or yours? Let me tell you, you cannot get away by throwing the blame at the Ministry of Health. Let me tell you why: Because biodiesel companies, yes, they spray, but they cannot use fumigants. Fumigation is under your control, under the control of the Ministry of Agriculture. Everyone knows that fumigants are widely used in public health disinfection. Who will supervise this, who will supervise it? The Ministry of Health will inspect food, the Ministry of Health will inspect public health pesticides, and the Ministry of Agriculture will inspect fumigants.

Which one have you inspected so far, which pesticide application? The man is in the room,
The moulder master uses fumigant and people die with the fumigant used by the moulder master. Is it just those 4 people who died due to fumigation?
Well, doesn't this ever come to your mind? There are a lot of agricultural engineers, if you gather them together and establish a company for them.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (Istanbul) - If you give public support, they will be able to use these fumigants properly.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Thank you, Mr. Good Morning.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Let me finish if you want.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Make your greeting.
ÖMER FETHI GÜRER (Niğde) - You should also enter the chap, my President.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - There is a lot to cover in twenty, twenty-two minutes.
AHMET VEHBİ BAKIRLIOĞLU (Manisa) - We were afraid that we would not be able to go out.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - So let me say this in summary: This booklet is nothing but fairy tales for adults. I told you these. Whether you buy these, you say, "An agricultural engineer friend of ours told us these, is there any truth to them?" with the bureaucrats behind you.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Dear Nejla Demir, I will give you my word.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - I will finish immediately, Mr. President.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Please. We apply it very clearly here.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - I am finishing, Mr. President. If you want, I can leave it immediately. So I'm finishing my speech.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Please finish, but there is a system that we all apply here with our friends, so it is not something special for you.
Finish it, go ahead.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - I recommend that you get the solution to this together with your friends.
And - I don't want to be arrogant - there is a book like this: Here the facts are written, here are the things that an agricultural engineer of forty years has seen in Turkish agriculture with his friends. Instead of believing this booklet, come, take a look at this and evaluate your two and a half years of Ministry of Agriculture.
I will end my last words with this: Look, there have been 8 Ministers of Agriculture so far. One of them is an agricultural engineer and one is a veterinarian; One of the remaining is an economist, the other is a journalist, and the other is a graduate of the Higher Islamic Institute. Well, just learning the terminology of agriculture takes years.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Dear Mr. Good Morning, I gave you additional time to greet you, please.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Therefore, at least appoint a manager among you who knows the subject so that we can talk to them.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Ms. Nejla Demir.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - In this context, I respectfully greet all my friends. I offer my respects in the hope of making progress in solving the problems of agriculture and peasants in Türkiye together.
Thank you.

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (İstanbul) - Teşekkür ederim.
Evet, ben de sözlerimin başlangıcında, Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisi Başkan Vekilimiz sevgili dostum Sırrı Süreyya Önder'in kaybından dolayı duyduğumuz hem kişisel hem de partimiz adına büyük üzüntüyü burada bir kere daha ifade etmeyi gerekli görüyorum. Beraber çalışmak hepimiz için öğretici bir süreçti. Zor geceleri kolaylaştıran üslubu hiç aklımızdan çıkmayacak. Türkiye'nin bütünleşmesine, birleşmesine, barışa olan katkıları unutulmayacak. Kendisine rahmet diliyorum, DEM PARTİ'ye ve yakınlarına tekrar başsağlığı diliyorum.
Aynı zamanda, Sayın Genel Başkanımıza yönelik yapılan alçakça saldırıyı isim vererek kınayan tüm Komisyon üyelerine de teşekkürlerimizi ifade etmek istiyorum.
Evet, gerçekten üç erkten biri yasama, diğer ikisi yürütme ve yargı. Yargının bağımsızlığı ve tarafsızlığının devlet olmanın çok önemli bir unsuru olduğunu biliyoruz. İki tane önemli laf var: Birincisi, "Devletin dini adalettir." lafı ama en az onun kadar önemli ve milattan sonra 50'nci yılda söylenmiş bir başka söz daha var, "Devletin içerisinden adaleti çıkartırsanız bir çeteden başka ne kalır ki?" diyor. Bunu milattan sonra 50'nci yılda bir felsefeci söylemiş. Dolayısıyla yasama, yürütme ve yargı arasındaki erkler ayrılığının sağlanması açısından önemli bir toplantıyı gerçekleştiriyoruz.
Şimdi, arkadaşlarımız çeşitli sözler söylediler, Türkiye'deki yargının bağımsızlığına ve tarafsızlığa ilişkin açıklamalar yaptılar, kendi dönemlerinde buna ne kadar önem verildiğini ifade ettiler.
Efendim, Allah rahmet eylesin, Bolu'daki otel yangınında çocuğunu kaybeden Danıştay Daire Başkanımızın bu Meclis çatısı altında yaptığı konuşma hepimiz için öğreticidir. O demişti ki: "Biz aldığımızda yargıya güven yüzde 80'ler düzeyindeydi, şimdi yargıya güven yüzde 20'lere düştü. Bu, hepimiz için ders alıcı bir durum ve nitelik taşımaktadır." Ben bunu mevcut yüksek yargının önemli bir daire başkanının sözü üzerinden burada aktarmayı gerekli görüyorum.

nelere yol açıyor, onu da ifade edelim. Sonra aynı hâkim, hâlen kürsü kullanan hâkim "Bana Ekrem İmamoğlu'na yönelik yargılamada ceza vermem konusunda baskı yapıldı. Ceza vermezsem başıma gelecekler konusunda çeşitli şeyler söylendi ve üstelik de ceza verirsem kararın istinafta onaylanacağına yönelik de garanti verildi." dedi. GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (İstanbul) - Arkadaşlar, bu gereksiz laf atmalara yanıt vermeye gerek yok çünkü doğru bir açıklamayı doğru bir üslupla yapıyoruz.
Bu, yargılamaya külliyen gölge düşüren bir husustur. Hâkimler ve Savcılar Kurulu araştıracak, bunu ben araştırmayacağım. HSK gidecek, hâkimin doğru söyleyip söylemediğine ilişkin bir inceleme ve değerlendirme yapacak, HSK'nin 2'nci Dairesinin görevi tam olarak budur. Bırakın resen bunu yapmayı, yaptığımız başvuru üzerine dahi bunu reddediyor ve biz bu yargılamayı hâlâ istinafta sürdürüyoruz. Sonra burada bizim yargımızın bağımsız ve tarafsız olduğu konusunda çeşitli konuşmaları da dinlemeye devam ediyoruz. Şimdi, burada yapacağımız oylama da Komisyonda yaptığımız uygulama da bu zihniyetin bir devamı olarak kendisini gösteriyor. Bunu üzülerek ifade etmek istiyorum.
Şimdi, Mecliste milletin iradesiyle ortaya çıkmış sandalye sayısı var. O sandalye sayısına uygun bir dağılım yalnızca partilerin orada kullandığı oylarla değil, milletin iradesinin yargıya yansımasını gösterecektir ve bu iradeyi biz adil yansıtmak zorundayız. Üstelik, bu bizim subjektif kararımızdan öte Anayasa’nın amir hükmüdür.

Gerçekten, Komisyonda yapılacak oylamanın usulüne ilişkin Anayasa'da da İç Tüzük'te de hüküm eksikliği vardır ancak aynı oylamanın Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisi Genel Kurulunda nasıl yapılacağını Anayasa’nın 159'uncu maddesi açıkça dercetmiştir. Arkadaşlar ifade ettiler, onlardan bir kısmında Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi grubu uygun bulmadığı için oylamaya katılmadı, bir diğerinde ise ortaya çıkan sonucun bir uzlaşıyla olduğu için buna ilişkin itirazını dercetti ve öyle uygulama yapıldı.
Şimdi, biz ne yapmaya çalışıyoruz? Biz, çok daha az milletvekilline sahip olan parti ile onun 2,5 katı, 3 katı milletvekiline sahip olan partiye aynı derecede, aynı sayıda HSK üyesi vermek üzere usulü Anayasa'ya aykırı olarak buraya dercetmeye çalışıyoruz. Bu, yalnızca bir hâkim falan değildir. O hâkimin adı Ahmet olsa ne olur, Mehmet olsa ne olur? Biz burada Anayasa’nın amir hükmüne uygun bir karar alamaz da ad çekme usulüyle bunu yapacak duruma Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisini getirirsek, bu, Meclisin saygınlığı açısından da doğru bir tutum olmaz.
Kendi adıma hep söylediğimi bir kere daha söyleyeyim: Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi iktidarında kürsüde bir tek CHP'li hâkim ve savcı olmamalıdır. Bu, diğer partilerin iktidarında da aynı olmalıdır. Biz karşısına çıktığımız hâkimin, savcının tarafsız ve bağımsız olduğundan emin olursak, bu memlekette devlete olan güvenimiz, adalete olan güvenimiz birazcık daha artar. Bunu hep beraber sağlamanın yararlı olacağını düşünüyorum ve Komisyon üyesi olmamama karşın söz verdiğiniz için teşekkür ediyorum, sağ olun.

GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (Istanbul) - Thank you.
Yes, at the beginning of my remarks, I find it necessary to express here once again the great sadness we feel, both personally and on behalf of our party, for the loss of our dear friend Sırrı Süreyya Önder, Deputy Speaker of the Grand National Assembly of Turkey. Working together was an instructive process for all of us. We will never forget his style that made difficult nights easier. His contributions to Türkiye's integration, unification and peace will not be forgotten. I wish him mercy and offer my condolences again to DEM PARTY and his relatives.
At the same time, I would like to express my gratitude to all the members of the Commission who condemned the vile attack on our President by naming them.
Yes, indeed, one of the three powers is the legislature, the other two are the executive and the judiciary. We know that the independence and impartiality of the judiciary is a very important element of being a state. There are two important words: First, "The religion of the state is justice." But there is another saying that is at least as important and was said in the 50th year of Christ: "If you remove justice from the state, what is left other than a gang?" he says. A philosopher said this in the 50th year of Christ. Therefore, we are holding an important meeting in terms of ensuring the separation of powers between the legislature, executive and judiciary.
Now, our friends made various remarks, made statements about the independence and impartiality of the judiciary in Türkiye, and expressed how much importance was given to this in their period.
Sir, may God have mercy on him, the speech made by the Head of the Council of State Department, who lost his child in the hotel fire in Bolu, under the roof of this Parliament is instructive for all of us. He said: "When we took office, trust in the judiciary was at the level of 80 percent, now trust in the judiciary has dropped to 20 percent. This is a situation and a lesson for all of us." I find it necessary to convey this here through the words of an important department head of the current higher judiciary.
I would like to say and express one more issue here. The framework of the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye is an important framework where we must emphasize the facts clearly. There is the Minister of Justice within the Council of Judges and Prosecutors. It existed in the past during the DSP period, but it was not removed. I swear, DSP is currently your coalition or government partner, so DSP will come out and say why it did this or why it did not do this, but this cannot be defended here as a reason for not abolishing the practice that has directly harmed the independence of this judiciary for twenty-two years.
Well, this
Let's explain what it causes. For example, while the trial was continuing in the Ekrem İmamoğlu trial, you sent a judge, who had not yet completed his first year in Istanbul, back to Samsun, where he came from, despite the principle of geographical guarantee, despite his wife being pregnant, and despite his petitions that he did not want it. Then the same judge, who still uses the bench, said: "I was pressured to impose a penalty in the trial against Ekrem İmamoğlu. Various things were said about what would happen to me if I did not impose a penalty, and moreover, a guarantee was given that if I imposed a penalty, the decision would be approved at the appeal." he said.

Now, I do not know whether the judge working in Samsun is telling the truth or not, no one knows, but this should be investigated by the HSK, and the 2nd Chamber of the HSK was established for this purpose. If.
HALUK İPEK (Amasya) - Then why are you saying it here? Why do you say it then?
BÜLENT TEZCAN (Aydın) - He will say it here. Where will he say it? He will say it here!
TURAN TAŞKIN ÖZER (Istanbul) - Look, while you were talking, no one made a comment, you are making comments.
HALUK İPEK (Amasya) - He did it, he did it.
BÜLENT TEZCAN (Aydın) - He will say it here. Where will he say it?
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Friends, there is no need to respond to these unnecessary quibbles because we are making the right statement in the right style.
This is an issue that completely casts a shadow over the trial. The Council of Judges and Prosecutors will investigate, I will not investigate this. The HSK will go and make an examination and evaluation as to whether the judge is telling the truth or not. This is exactly the duty of the 2nd Chamber of the HSK. Let alone doing this ex officio, he refuses to do so even upon our application, and we are still continuing this trial in appeal. Then we continue to listen to various speeches about the independence and impartiality of our judiciary. Now, the vote we will make here and the practice we take in the Commission show themselves as a continuation of this mentality. I want to express this with regret.
Now, there is a number of seats in the Parliament determined by the will of the people. A distribution appropriate to that number of seats will reflect the will of the nation in the judiciary, not just the votes cast by the parties there, and we must reflect this will fairly. Moreover, this is a mandatory provision of the Constitution rather than our subjective decision.
Look, our friends say it, and they express it correctly. Indeed, there is a lack of provision in both the Constitution and the Internal Regulations regarding the procedure for voting in the Commission, but Article 159 of the Constitution clearly states how the same vote will be held in the General Assembly of the Turkish Grand National Assembly. Then, in accordance with the principle of parallelism of method and procedure, shouldn't we apply the same method in the Commission as we would in the General Assembly? Oh, you might say, "Well, we didn't do it last time." Friends stated that some of them did not participate in the voting because the Republican People's Party group did not find it appropriate, while others objected to the result because it was a consensus, and the implementation was carried out accordingly.
Now, what are we trying to do? We are trying to have the procedure included here, contrary to the Constitution, in order to give the same degree and the same number of HSK members to the party that has much fewer deputies and the party that has 2.5 or 3 times more deputies. This is not just a judge or anything. What if that judge's name was Ahmet, what if it was Mehmet? If we cannot take a decision in accordance with the mandatory provision of the Constitution and put the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye in a position to do this by voting, this would not be the right attitude in terms of the dignity of the Assembly.
Let me say once again what I have always said on my own behalf: In the Republican People's Party government, there should not be a single CHP judge or prosecutor on the bench.

This should be the same when other parties are in power. If we are sure that the judge or prosecutor we appear before is impartial and independent, our trust in the state and justice in this country will increase a little more. I think it would be useful to achieve this together, and I thank you for your commitment even though I am not a member of the Commission. Thank you.

Let's explain what it causes. For example, while the trial was continuing in the Ekrem İmamoğlu trial, you sent a judge, who had not yet completed his first year in Istanbul, back to Samsun, where he came from, despite the principle of geographical guarantee, despite his wife being pregnant, and despite his petitions that he did not want it. Then the same judge, who still uses the bench, said: "I was pressured to impose a penalty in the trial against Ekrem İmamoğlu. Various things were said about what would happen to me if I did not impose a penalty, and moreover, a guarantee was given that if I imposed a penalty, the decision would be approved at the appeal." he said.

Now, I do not know whether the judge working in Samsun is telling the truth or not, no one knows, but this should be investigated by the HSK, and the 2nd Chamber of the HSK was established for this purpose. If.
HALUK İPEK (Amasya) - Then why are you saying it here? Why do you say it then?
BÜLENT TEZCAN (Aydın) - He will say it here. Where will he say it? He will say it here!
TURAN TAŞKIN ÖZER (Istanbul) - Look, while you were talking, no one made a comment, you are making comments.
HALUK İPEK (Amasya) - He did it, he did it.
BÜLENT TEZCAN (Aydın) - He will say it here. Where will he say it?
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Friends, there is no need to respond to these unnecessary quibbles because we are making the right statement in the right style.
This is an issue that completely casts a shadow over the trial. The Council of Judges and Prosecutors will investigate this, I will not investigate this. The HSK will go and make an examination and evaluation as to whether the judge is telling the truth or not. This is exactly the duty of the 2nd Chamber of the HSK. Let alone doing this ex officio, he refuses to do so even upon our application, and we are still continuing this trial in appeal. Then we continue to listen to various speeches about the independence and impartiality of our judiciary. Now, both the vote we will make here and the practice we take in the Commission show themselves as a continuation of this mentality. I want to express this with regret.
Now, there is a number of seats in the Parliament determined by the will of the people. A distribution appropriate to that number of seats will reflect the will of the nation in the judiciary, not just the votes cast by the parties there, and we must reflect this will fairly. Moreover, this is a mandatory provision of the Constitution rather than our subjective decision.
Look, our friends say it, and they express it correctly. Indeed, there is a lack of provision in both the Constitution and the Internal Regulations regarding the procedure for voting in the Commission, but Article 159 of the Constitution clearly states how the same vote will be held in the General Assembly of the Turkish Grand National Assembly. Then, in accordance with the principle of parallelism of method and procedure, shouldn't we apply the same method in the Commission as we would in the General Assembly? Oh, you might say, "Well, we didn't do it last time." Friends stated that some of them did not participate in the voting because the Republican People's Party group did not find it appropriate, while others objected to the result because it was a consensus, and the implementation was carried out accordingly.
Now, what are we trying to do? We are trying to have the procedure included here, contrary to the Constitution, in order to give the same degree and the same number of HSK members to the party that has much fewer deputies and the party that has 2.5 or 3 times more deputies. This is not just a judge or anything. What if that judge's name was Ahmet, what if it was Mehmet? If we cannot take a decision in accordance with the mandatory provision of the Constitution and put the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye in a position to do this by voting, this would not be the right attitude in terms of the dignity of the Assembly.
Let me say once again what I have always said on my own behalf: In the Republican People's Party government, there should not be a single CHP judge or prosecutor on the bench.

This should be the same when other parties are in power. If we are sure that the judge or prosecutor we appear before is impartial and independent, our trust in the state and justice in this country will increase a little more. I think it would be useful to achieve this together, and I thank you for your commitment even though I am not a member of the Commission. Thank you.
